sources.title: AI In Sports Market Share, Size, Trends, Report 2026
sources.claim: AI in sports market worth $9.76B in 2026, growing at 27.85% CAGR toward $33.32B by 2031.

sources.title: AI in Sports Market Size, Share and Trends 2026 to 2035
sources.claim: Global AI in sports market projected to surpass $27B–$33B by 2030–2031 at ~28–30% CAGR; North America leads with the largest regional share.

sources.title: The Future of Football Is Here: AI Solutions To Power FIFA World Cup 2026™
sources.claim: Lenovo named FIFA's Official Technology Partner for World Cup 2026; deploying Football AI Pro (trained on 2,000+ metrics, millions of FIFA data points) to all 48 national teams; edge AI infrastructure for real-time broadcast and operational support across 16 stadiums.

sources.title: Faster offside decisions, referee body cams and more analysis: innovation at FIFA World Cup 2026™
sources.claim: 16 optical tracking cameras per stadium generating 150M data points per match; 3D player avatars scanned in ~1 second; instantaneous AI-assisted VAR offside decisions delivered directly to assistant referees on the pitch — first use at a FIFA World Cup.

sources.title: AI in Sports Analytics Market — CAGR of 33.0%
sources.claim: AI in sports analytics segment growing at 33% CAGR; $0.9B in 2023 to $15.6B by 2033. Broader AI in sports at 30.3% CAGR per separate Market.us report.

sources.title: AI's World Cup debut: real-time data poised to reshape football strategy
sources.claim: 82% of sports organizations have adopted AI as of 2026; 75% of professional teams rely on real-time analytics for performance and strategy; 3 in 4 report tangible financial results.

sources.title: World Cup 2026 sparks a $11bn transformation
sources.claim: FIFA World Cup 2026 projected to generate $10.9B in revenue (nearly double Qatar 2022); $320M in incremental analytics platform investments across participating nations.

sources.title: Why More Teams Are Switching to AI-Powered Sports Analytics
sources.claim: WSC Sports delivers 150M+ AI-generated clips per year to 160+ sports rights holders; claims 4x engagement uplift from AI video vs. human-edited clips.
sources.note: Source is WSC Sports company blog — figures directionally reliable but commercially motivated; treat engagement data as self-reported

sources.title: Sportradar Group AG — Form 6-K FY2026 Q1 (SEC filing)
sources.claim: Sportradar Q1 2026 revenue €347M (+11% YoY). Q4 2025 revenue €369M (+20% YoY).

sources.title: Genius Sports Ltd — Form 6-K FY2025 (SEC filing)
sources.claim: Genius Sports FY2025 total group revenue $669.5M — an increase of $158.6M year-over-year.

sources.title: What the FIFA World Cup 2026 Reveals About Enterprise AI at Scale
sources.claim: Lenovo's Football AI Pro trained on millions of FIFA-owned data points and 2,000+ football-specific metrics; provides live substitution and tactical suggestions based on fatigue markers.

sources.title: How AI & Sports Analytics Are Transforming Every Sport in 2026
sources.claim: Computer vision tracks players 20 times per second; wearable sensors measure exertion at the cellular level; neural networks process billions of recorded sports events.
sources.note: Medium blog post — use as qualitative context only; specific technical figures should be cross-referenced with primary vendor sources [unverified]

unverified_claims.claim: Catapult Sports used by 68% of Premier League clubs
unverified_claims.note: Widely cited in sports analytics coverage but no primary source found with exact survey methodology. Catapult's website claims 3,500+ elite teams across 40+ sports. [unverified — treat as directional]

unverified_claims.claim: AI injury prevention saves NFL franchises $1–3M per avoided ACL surgery
unverified_claims.note: Derived from public estimates of player replacement and lost performance costs. No single authoritative source published this specific figure. [unverified — calculated estimate]

unverified_claims.claim: Sportradar holds ~15% market share by revenue in AI in sports
unverified_claims.note: Calculated by dividing Sportradar's ~$1.5B annual revenue against The Business Research Company's $9.76B total market. Assumes both figures use comparable market definitions, which is uncertain. [unverified — calculated estimate]

unverified_claims.claim: Sports betting legal in 38 U.S. states creating $10B+ annual wagering market
unverified_claims.note: State-level legalization count is directionally accurate as of mid-2025 but exact state count varies by source due to ongoing legislative activity. Wagering market size ($10B+) is widely reported but varies significantly by source definition (gross vs. net revenue). [partially verified]
